headline: Israel turns its back on France as Paris struggles to maintain dialogue
description: The Israeli Ministry of Defense announced on Tuesday that it would end its defense imports from France, implying that Paris was no longer counted among the country's allies. This move comes despite French hopes of repairing diplomatic ties damaged by France's recognition of the State of Palestine and amid the ongoing war in Lebanon.

headline: The deadly cheetah trafficking between Somaliland and the Gulf
Israeli army says it 'struck senior Hezbollah commander' in Beirut
French archives of Spaniards who fled Franco's dictatorship are now available online
description: From March 30, over 180,000 records are accessible on the French Office for the Protection of Refugees and Stateless Persons' website, due to funding from Spain aimed at preserving the memory of people's 40 years of exile in France.

headline: France's far right splits over the question of sovereignty as several mayors take down EU flags
description: Several newly elected mayors from far-right leader Marine Le Pen's party have removed the European Union flag, blue with gold stars, from their town halls. The decision has sparked divisions between the nationalists.

headline: Robert Capa, a photographer in the heat of the action, at Paris's Liberation Museum
description: A compelling exhibition, which features a montage of archival footage showing the work of Robert Capa, the Hungarian cofounder of Magnum Photos, details his extraordinary life.

headline: Europeans remain powerless to tackle Orban, seen as too close to the Kremlin
description: With Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orban, who has close ties with the Kremlin, vetoing new aid to Ukraine, European leaders hope their relations with Budapest will return to normal after Hungary's April 12 parliamentary elections.
byline: Jean-Baptiste Chastand,  Philippe Jacqué and  Virginie Malingre

War is transforming Ukraine's dogs who live on the front line
description: Canines found near combat zones are small, light and dark-colored, according to a study. These characteristics have helped them survive in a hostile environment.

headline: 'StravaLeaks': How Le Monde located 18,000 French military personnel with a fitness app
description: For more than two years, Le Monde has been tracking a significant proportion of France's military personnel on Strava, a popular fitness app, on almost a daily basis. Their movements can be monitored, including in sensitive areas, putting both military operations and the servicemembers involved in them at risk.

headline: Spice up your life! Five Paris restaurants where you can eat spicy food
description: Mexican chipotle, Korean kimchi, Tunisian harissa: Whether mild or fiery, these condiments add zest to dishes from around the world. Here are five addresses in Paris and Montreuil to awaken your taste buds.
